title_en: Chapter 12: The Bid`ah invented by Quraish by calling themselves as
title_id: Bab 12: Bid'ah yang Diciptakan Quraisy dengan Menyebut Diri Mereka al-Hums
content_id:
Al-Hums berarti: 'keras dan taat beragama secara ketat.' Quraisy mendapat sebutan demikian karena mereka berlebihan dalam mengagungkan al-Haram (Tanah Suci) dan melampaui batas. Dan karena ini, mereka mewajibkan diri mereka sendiri untuk tidak pergi ke Arafah (yang berada di luar batas al-Haram). Mereka biasa berkata: 'Kita adalah orang-orang al-Haram dan penghuni Rumah Allah.' Sehingga mereka tidak pergi ke Arafah, dalam ketaatan pada inovasi mereka yang korup ini, meskipun mereka tahu betul bahwa itu adalah bagian dari haji yang diwajibkan oleh Ibrahim 'alaihis salam. Dan mereka biasa melarang para jamaah haji — mereka yang datang untuk haji atau umrah — dari makan kecuali makanan yang dipersiapkan oleh Quraisy, dan mereka biasa melarang mereka melakukan tawaf mengelilingi Ka'bah kecuali dengan menggunakan pakaian yang disediakan oleh mereka. Sehingga jika salah seorang di antara mereka tidak menemukan pakaian yang disediakan oleh al-Hums, ia akan melakukan tawaf dalam keadaan telanjang, bahkan jika itu adalah seorang wanita. Ibn Ishaq berkata: Mereka biasa melakukan itu hingga Allah mengutus Muhammad shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, dan menurunkan kepadanya Al-Qur'an, dan membantah mereka dalam hal-hal yang mereka bid'ahkan, firman Allah: 'Kemudian bertolaklah kamu dari tempat bertolaknya orang-orang banyak dan mohonlah ampun kepada Allah; sesungguhnya Allah Maha Pengampun lagi Maha Penyayang.' [Surah al-Baqarah (2): 199] Maknanya adalah: bertolaklah dari Arafah dari mana mayoritas orang bertolak. Dan kami (Ibn Kathir) telah menyebutkan sebelumnya bahwa Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam biasa tinggal di Arafah (pada Hari Arafah bersama para jamaah haji lainnya), bahkan sebelum beliau menerima Wahyu. Ini adalah dari petunjuk dan perlindungan Allah baginya shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam.

\n

Dan Allah Ta'ala menurunkan kepadanya bantahan terhadap mereka dan praktik bid'ah mereka berupa melarang orang-orang dari makan dan mengenakan pakaian. Allah Ta'ala berfirman: 'Hai anak Adam, pakailah pakaianmu yang indah di setiap (memasuki) masjid, makan dan minumlah, dan janganlah berlebih-lebihan. Sesungguhnya Allah tidak menyukai orang-orang yang berlebih-lebihan. Katakanlah: Siapakah yang mengharamkan perhiasan dari Allah yang telah dikeluarkan-Nya untuk hamba-hamba-Nya dan (siapa pulakah yang mengharamkan) rezeki yang baik?' [Surah al-A'raf (7): 31-32] Ibn Abbas radhiyallahu 'anhuma berkata: 'Para wanita biasa melakukan tawaf mengelilingi Ka'bah dalam keadaan telanjang, sambil berkata: Hari ini sebagian atau semua itu akan tampak, dan apa pun yang tampak aku tidak menjadikannya halal. Kemudian ayat berikut diturunkan: Hai anak Adam, pakailah pakaianmu yang indah di setiap (memasuki) masjid.' 'Urwah meriwayatkan: 'Pada masa Jahiliyyah (masa kebodohan pra-Islam), orang-orang biasa melakukan tawaf Ka'bah dalam keadaan telanjang kecuali al-Hums; dan al-Hums adalah Quraisy dan keturunan mereka. Al-Hums biasa memberikan pakaian kepada para pria yang melakukan tawaf dengan mengenakannya, dan para wanita (dari al-Hums) biasa memberikan pakaian kepada para wanita yang melakukan tawaf dengan mengenakannya. Mereka yang al-Hums tidak memberi pakaian akan melakukan tawaf mengelilingi Ka'bah dalam keadaan telanjang. Kebanyakan orang biasa pergi langsung dari Arafah, tetapi mereka (al-Hums) biasa berangkat setelah tinggal di Muzdalifah.'

\n

'Urwah menambahkan: 'Ayahku meriwayatkan bahwa 'Aisyah radhiyallahu 'anha berkata: Ayat berikut diturunkan tentang al-Hums: Kemudian bertolaklah kamu dari tempat bertolaknya orang-orang banyak. [Surah al-Baqarah (2): 199]' 'Urwah menambahkan: 'Quraisy dan orang-orang yang memeluk agama mereka biasa menyebut diri mereka sebagai al-Hums. Mereka biasa tinggal di Muzdalifah dan berangkat dari sana (ke Mina), sementara sisa orang-orang Arab biasa tinggal di Arafah. Maka mereka (al-Hums) diperintahkan untuk pergi ke Arafah (atas perintah Allah).' Perintah untuk melarang tawaf dalam keadaan telanjang diturunkan setahun sebelum Hajjatul-Wada' (haji perpisahan Nabi). Abu Hurairah radhiyallahu 'anhu berkata: 'Setahun sebelum haji terakhir Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam di mana beliau menunjuk Abu Bakr sebagai kepala para jamaah haji, Abu Bakr radhiyallahu 'anhu mengirimku bersama para pengumum pada Hari Nahr di Mina untuk mengumumkan: Tidak ada musyrik yang boleh melaksanakan haji setelah tahun ini, dan tidak ada yang boleh melakukan tawaf mengelilingi Ka'bah dalam keadaan telanjang.'
content_en:
al-Hums 74 "Hums" means: "stubborn and strictly religious". Quraish were so called because they exaggerated in the sanctity of al-Haram (The Sanctuary) and exceeded the limits. And because of this, they made it binding upon themselves not to go to `Arafah (which was outside of the boundaries of al-Haram). They used to say: "We are the people of alHaram and the inhabitants of the House of Allaah." So they would not go to `Arafah, in adherence to this corrupt innovation of theirs, even though they knew well that it was part of the pilgrimage which was enjoined by Ibraaheem عليه السالم. And they used to prevent the pilgrims – those who came for Hajj or `Umrah – from eating except the food that was prepared by Quraish, and they used to prevent them from doing the Tawaaf around the Ka`bah except in the clothes provided by them. So if one of them did not find clothes provided by the Hums, he would do the Tawaaf naked, even if it happened to be a woman. So the woman who did the Tawaaf naked used to place her hand over her private parts and say: "Today some or all of it will appear, and whatever appears I don't make is permissible." Ibn Is-haaq said: They used to do that till Allaah sent Muhammad ﷺ, and revealed to him the Qur’aan, and refuted them in the things that which they innovated, saying: ﴿ َّ ث أ ف يض وا م ن ح ي ث أ ف اض النَّاس و اس تـ غ ف ر وا اللَّـه ۖ َّإ ن اللَّـه غ ف ور رَّح يم﴾ {Then depart from the place whence all the people depart}, meaning: depart from `Arafah from where the majority of the people depart. {and ask Allaah for His Forgiveness. Truly, Allaah is Oft-Forgiving, Most-Merciful.} [Surah al-Baqarah (2): 199] And we (Ibn Katheer) have already mentioned that the Prophet ﷺ used to stay in `Arafah (on the Day of `Arafah with the other pilgrims), even before he received Revelation. This is from the guidance and protection of Allaah for him ﷺ. 74 [TN: The meaning and some narrations were already mentioned in chapter 8, and how they refused to go to `Arafah during Hajj because it was outside the boundaries of al-Haram.]

\n

And Allaah تعاىل revealed to him the refutation against them and their innovated practice of forbidding the people from eating and wearing clothes. Allaah تعاىل says: ﴿ي ا ب ن آد م خ ذ وا ز ين ت ك م ع ند ِّك ل م س ج د و ك ل وا و اش ر ب وا و ّل ت س ر ف وا ۖ إ نَّه ّل ُُّي ب ال م س ر ف ني * ق ل م ن ح رَّم ز ين ة اللَّـه الَّت أ خ ر ج ل ع ب اد ه و الطَّيِّب ات م ن الرِّز ق﴾ {O Children of Aadam! Take your adornment to every Masjid (by wearing your clean clothes for prayers and while doing Tawaaf around the Ka`bah), and eat and drink but waste not by extravagance; certainly, He (Allaah) likes not Al-Musrifoon (those who waste by extravagance). Say: "Who has forbidden the adornment of Allaah which He has produced for His servants and the good [lawful] things of provision?"} [Surah alA`raaf (7): 31-32] [Shaikh al-Albaanee’s addition] Ibn `Abbaas رضي اهلل عنهما said: ❝Women used to circumambulate the Ka`bah naked, saying: ‘Today some or all of it will appear, and whatever appears I don't make is permissible.’ Then the following Aayah was revealed: {O Children of Aadam! Take your adornment to every Masjid…}❞ 75 Narrated `Urwa: ❝During the Jaahiliyyah (pre-Islamic period of ignorance), the people used to perform Tawaaf of the Ka`bah naked except al-Hums; and the Hums were the Quraish and their offspring. The Hums used to give clothes to the men who would perform the Tawaaf wearing them, and the women (of the Hums) used to give clothes to the women who would perform the Tawaaf wearing them. Those to whom the Hums 75 sahih Muslim (3028-25) and Sunan al-Nasaa’ee (2956) [TN: The command prohibiting the performance of the Tawaaf naked was revealed a year prior to Hajjatul-Wadaa` (the Prophet’s farewell pilgrimage). Narrated Humaid bin `Abdul-Rahmaan: Abu Hurairah رضي اهلل عنه said: ❝In the year prior to the last Hajj of the Prophet ﷺ in which he ﷺ appointed Abu Bakr as the chief of the pilgrims, Abu Bakr رضي اهلل عنه sent me (Abu Hurairah) along with announcers on the Day of Nahr in Mina to announce: "No Mushrik (pagan) shall perform Hajj after this year, and none shall perform the Tawaaf around the Ka`bah in a naked state."❞ Humaid bin `AbdulRahmaan added: "Then Allaah’s Messenger صلى اهلل عليه وسلم sent `Alee bin Abu Taalib رضي اهلل عنه (after Abu Bakr) and ordered him to recite aloud in public Surah al-Bara’ah (Surah al-Tawbah)." Abu Hurairah رضي اهلل عنه added: "So `Alee, along with us, recited Surah al-Bara’ah (loudly) before the people at Mina on the Day of Nahr and announced: ❝ "No pagan shall perform Hajj after this year and none shall perform the Tawaaf around the Ka`bah in a naked state."❞ [sahih al-Bukhaaree (369, 1622, 3177, 4363, 4655, 4656, 4657) and sahih Muslim (1347-435)]]

\n

did not give clothes would perform Tawaaf around the Ka`bah naked. Most of the people used to go away (disperse) directly from `Arafah but they (al-Hums) used to depart after staying at al-Muzdalifah.❞ `Urwa added: ❝My father narrated that `Aaishah رضي اهلل عنها had said: "The following Aayah was revealed about the Hums: ﴿ َّ ث أ ف يض وا م ن ح ي ث أ ف اض النَّاس﴾ {Then depart from the place whence all the people depart} [Surah al-Baqarah (2): 199]" ❞ `Urwa added: ❝The Quraish and those who embraced their religion used to call themselves as al-Hums. They used to stay at al-Muzdalifah and used to depart from there (to Mina), while the rest of the Arabs used to stay at `Arafah. So they (al-Hums) were sent to `Arafah (by Allaah’s order).❞ 76 [End of Shaikh al-Albaanee’s addition] 76 [sahih al-Bukhaaree (1665, 4520) and sahih Muslim (1219 – 151 & 152)]
